title: lepechinia tomentosa
intro: Lepechinia tomentosa, also known as Catmint or Mountain Catmint, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Lepechinia tomentosa include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for easy consumption and utilization of its medicinal properties.
